Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Paris Olympics 2024: Meet South Africa’s TRIATHLON team

South Africa will be represented by three competitors in the men’s and women’s triathlon competition at the Paris Olympics 2024.

South Africa will be represented bythreecompetitors in the men’s and women’striathloncompetition at theParis Olympics 2024.

For the latest Paris Olympics 2024 news, bookmarkThe South African website’sdedicated section

Henri SchoemanandJamie Riddlewill battle for the medals in the men’s division, whileVicky van der Merwewill be Mzansi’s lone women’s entrant.

The triathlon race format at the Olympics for both the women’s and men’s events consist of a 1 500m swim, a 40km cycle and a 10km run.

There are no heats; the competition is a single race in which the first athlete to cross the finish line will be the gold medal winner.

A mixed relay event was added at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics, which saw teams of two men and two women compete over a short‑course triathlon (each athlete performs a triathlon of 300m swim, 8km cycle and a 2km run in a relay format).

All three events will return at the 2024 Paris Olympics.

South Africa willnothave a team in the mixed relay event.

A total of110athletes are expected to compete atPont Alexandre III.

The men’s competitionwill take placeon Tuesday, 30 July with the women’s competition scheduled for a day later.

The mixed competition will be contested on Monday, 5 August.

All three races will take place at08:00(SA time).

Triathlon was added to the Olympic programme at the 2000 Sydney Olympics.

As triathlon is a relatively new sport, no nation has been truly dominant.

The 39 medals awarded in the six Games to date have been shared between 16 National Olympic Committees, none of which has won more than three gold medals (Great Britain).

South Africa has wonone bronzemedal to date, thanks to Henri Schoeman at the 2016 Rio de Janeiro Olympics.

As reported byThe South African website, theSouth African Sports Confederation, Olympic and Paralympic Committee (Sascoc)has confirmed the prize money on offer for any South African medal winners – as well as their coaches.

– Athletes who win gold will be awardedR400 000, with the coach receivingR100 000

– Silver medalists will earnR200 000andR50 000for the coach

– Bronze medal payouts will beR75 000andR25 000for the athlete and coach, respectively

Men:Henri Schoeman, Jamie Riddle

Women:Vicky van der Merwe

Management:Riana Robertson, Lindsey Parry
